name: HtmlTemplate

values.name: addOutstandingPagesAndTemplates
values.comment: Call this to initialize the list of toplevel templates and pages you need to render your application's first page. You don't need to mention everything, since the `HtmlTemplate` module will find pages and templates referenced by those you provide here, and call the `templateLoader` and and `pageLoader` functions you provided in your call to `makeLoaders` to load them.
values.type: List String -> List String -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: addPageProcessors
values.comment:
Sometimes you need to do something when a page is loaded. If so, instead of checking for that after your call to receivePage, you can ask `HtmlTemplate` to do the checking for you. The args to a page processor passed here are:

    pageName page loaders
    
After adding relevant state, your page processor returns the modified `loaders`.

If you add a page processor for a page with a blank name (`""`), it will be called for EVERY page that does not have an explicit page processor for its name.
values.type: List ( String, String -> Atom msg -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x ) -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: atomDecoder
values.comment: A `Json.Decode.Decoder` to parse JSON into an `Atom`. You will usually use `decodeAtom` for this, but if you have a data structure that includes an `Atom`, this allows you to include it in your own decoder.
values.type: Decoder (Atom msg)

values.name: atomEncoder
values.comment: Encode an `Atom` as a `Json.Encode.Value`. You will usually use `encodeAtom` to get a JSON `String`, but if you have a data structure that includes an `Atom`, this allows you to include it in your own encoder.
values.type: Atom msg -> Value

values.name: cantFuncall
values.comment:
Useful for reporting on badly-formed arguments to a function added with `insertFunctions` (or `insertMessages`). Turns the function call into a string, so that you can see that on your `render`ed web page.

I plan to eventually provide a delayed-evaluation mechanism, so that instead of turning a function call into a string, you can wrap it with that, but until then, this is what you've got.
values.type: String -> List (Atom msg) -> Atom msg

values.name: clearAtoms
values.comment: Removes all Atoms from the table inside of the `Loaders`. This function exists only as a mirror of `clearPages` and `clearTemplates`. You will rarely need to call it.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: clearPages
values.comment:
Removes all pages from the table inside of the `Loaders`. You will usually call `loadPage` right after this, to load the page the user just asked to display, and all the pages and templates it references.

The application in the `examples` directory calls this every time the page changes to force all changed pages to be reloaded.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: clearTemplates
values.comment:
Removes all templates from the table inside of the `Loaders`. You will usually call `loadPage` or `loadTemplate` right after this.

The application in the `examples` directory doesn't ever reload its templates, requiring a page refresh to do that, so it never calls `clearTemplates`.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: customEncodeAtom
values.comment:
Passes an `Atom` to `atomEncoder` and returns the resulting JSON `String`.

Convenience function. You can call `Json.Encode.encode` yourself, if you prefer.
values.type: Int -> Atom msg -> String

values.name: decodeAtom
values.comment:
Passes a JSON string to `atomDecoder` and return the `Result`.

Convenience function. You can call `Json.Decode.decodeString` yourself, if you prefer.
values.type: String -> Result String (Atom msg)

values.name: encodeAtom
values.comment:
Passes an `Atom` to `atomEncoder` and returns the resulting JSON `String`. Uses indentation of `1`. Call `customEncodeAtom` to specify the indentation yourself.

Convenience function. You can call `Json.Encode.encode` yourself, if you prefer.
values.type: Atom msg -> String

values.name: eval
values.comment:
This is exposed primarily so that the example application can use it on its `play` page. It can currently result in an `Atom` with function calls changed to strings and attributes already rendered into `Html`.

You shouldn't need to use it.
values.type: Atom msg -> Dicts msg -> Atom msg

values.name: getAtom
values.comment: Lookup the atom with the given name. Same as `"$<name>"` in a JSON file.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Maybe (Atom msg)

values.name: getDicts
values.comment: Get the `Dicts` out of a `Loaders`. Rarely needed.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> Dicts msg

values.name: getDictsAtom
values.comment:
Lookup an atom. Sometimes useful inside of funtions added with `insertFunctions` or `insertMessages`.

Same as `getAtom <| getDicts loaders`.
values.type: String -> Dicts msg -> Maybe (Atom msg)

values.name: getExtra
values.comment: If you use the `extra` arg to `makeLoaders` to put state there that you need in your page or template loader, you can fetch that state with this function.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> x

values.name: getPage
values.comment: Lookup the page with the given name. Same as `"@<name>"` in a JSON file.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Maybe (Atom msg)

values.name: getTemplate
values.comment: Lookup the template with the given name. Same as `"?<name>"` in a JSON file.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Maybe (Atom msg)

values.name: insertDelayedBindingsFunctions
values.comment: Some functions, e.g. variable binding functions like `"#let"` and `"#loop"`, and short-circuiting functions like `_if`, `"#&&"` and `"#||"`, need to delay evaluation of their arguments. If one of the functions you add with `insertFunctions` (or `insertMessages`) needs to be called with unevaluated arguments, declare that with `insertDelayedBindingsFunctions`.
values.type: List String -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: insertFunctions
values.comment:
If you want to define your own function to be available via "#foo" in your JSON files, you can do so with `insertFunctions` (or `insertMessages`).

There is more information about defining functions in the [JSON documentation](https://github.com/billstclair/elm-html-template/blob/master/JSON.md).
values.type: List ( String, List (Atom msg) -> Dicts msg -> Atom msg ) -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: insertMessages
values.comment:
If you need to script `onClick` or other event attributes that are processed by your `update` function, you'll need to define message functions to create them for your scripting code. Call `insertMessages` in your `init` function, with a list of those functions, before stashing the `Loaders` in your `Model`.

There are more details about message functions in the [JSON documentation](https://github.com/billstclair/elm-html-template/blob/master/JSON.md).
values.type: List ( String, List (Atom msg) -> Dicts msg -> msg ) -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: loadOutstandingPageOrTemplate
values.comment:
If there are pages or templates in the `Loaders` that have not been loaded, return a command to load one of them. Otherwise, return Cmd.none. You will usually call this in your `init` function and use the returned `Cmd` in the returned `( Model, Cmd Msg )`.

Calls the `templateLoader` or `pageLoader` you passed to `makeLoaders` to create the `Cmd`s.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg

values.name: loadPage
values.comment: Start the load of the page with the given name. Calls the `pageLoader` you used when you created the `Loaders` to get the returned `Cmd`. There's nothing special here. You could call that function yourself, or create the `Cmd` some other way.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg

values.name: loadTemplate
values.comment: Start the load of the template with the given name. Calls the `templateLoader` you used when you created the `Loaders` to get the returned `Cmd`. There's nothing special here. You could call that function yourself, or create the `Cmd` some other way.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg

values.name: makeLoaders
values.comment:
Call `makeLoaders` in your `init` function to create the object that stores state for `HtmlTemplate`:

    makeLoaders templateLoader pageLoader extra
    
`templateLoader name loaders` returns a Cmd to load a template, usually either from a file on the web server, via `Http`, or from a database.

`pageLoader name Loaders` does likewise for pages.

See the [JSON documentation](https://github.com/billstclair/elm-html-template/blob/master/JSON.md) for more about pages and templates.
values.type: (String -> Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg) -> (String -> Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg) -> x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: maybeLoadOutstandingPageOrTemplate
values.comment:
If there are pages or templates in the `Loaders` that have not been loaded, return a command to load one of them. Otherwise, return Nothing. At startup, applications will usually want to display a loading screen until Nothing comes back from here, at which time they can switch to the start page, since all of its templates and included pages will be in `Loaders`' tables then.

Calls the `templateLoader` or `pageLoader` you passed to `makeLoaders` to create the `Cmd`s.
values.type: Loaders msg x -> Maybe (Cmd msg)

values.name: receivePage
values.comment:
After your command to load a page has passed a `Msg` to your `update` function, and has some JSON in hand, call this function to decode that JSON into an `Atom`, and store it in `Loaders`' tables with the given name.

    receivePage name json loaders
    
The returned `Result` will be `Ok` with the modified `Loaders` if decoding was successful, or `Err msg`, where `msg` is the decoding error string otherwise.

Usually, you will then call `maybeLoadOutstandingPageOrTemplate` to continue loading referenced pages and templates.
values.type: String -> String -> Loaders msg x -> Result String (Loaders msg x)

values.name: receiveTemplate
values.comment:
After your command to load a template has passed a `Msg` to your `update` function, and has some JSON in hand, call this function to decode that JSON into an `Atom`, and store it in `Loaders`' tables with the given name.

    receiveTemplate name json loaders
    
The returned `Result` will be `Ok` with the modified `Loaders` if decoding was successful, or `Err msg`, where `msg` is the decoding error string otherwise.

Usually, you will then call `maybeLoadOutstandingPageOrTemplate` to continue loading referenced pages and templates.
values.type: String -> String -> Loaders msg x -> Result String (Loaders msg x)

values.name: removeAtom
values.comment: Remove an atom.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: removePage
values.comment: Remove a page.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: removeTemplate
values.comment: Remove a template.
values.type: String -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: render
values.comment:
Convert an `Atom` to `Html` for rendering a browser page. You will usually pass a template here, but since the only difference between a template, a page, or any other `Atom` is how you use them, that's up to you.

The application in the `examples` directory always passes its `page` template here (except on its `play` page, where it passes the `Atom` decoded from the user input).
values.type: Atom msg -> Loaders msg x -> Html msg

values.name: setAtom
values.comment: Store an atom with the given name. Done with `"#let"` in a JSON file.
values.type: String -> Atom msg -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: setAtoms
values.comment: Set multiple atoms. The same as calling `setAtom` for each pair.
values.type: List ( String, Atom msg ) -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: setExtra
values.comment: If you use the `extra` arg to `makeLoaders` to put state there that you need in your page or template loader, you can set that state with this function.
values.type: x -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: setPage
values.comment: Store a page with the given name.
values.type: String -> Atom msg -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: setPages
values.comment: Set multiple pages. The same as calling `setPage` for each pair.
values.type: List ( String, Atom msg ) -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: setTemplate
values.comment: Store a template with the given name.
values.type: String -> Atom msg -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: setTemplates
values.comment: Set multiple templates. The same as calling `setTemplate` for each pair.
values.type: List ( String, Atom msg ) -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x

values.name: tagWrap
values.comment:
Useful if a function added with `insertFunctions` wants to return a standard Html tag.

Same as `RecordAtom { tag = tag, attributes = attributes, body = body }`
values.type: String -> List ( String, Atom msg ) -> List (Atom msg) -> Atom msg

name: HtmlTemplate.Types

unions.name: Atom
unions.comment:
`Atom` is the basic data type of the `HtmlTemplate` module. JSON is parsed into an `Atom`, and an `Atom` is what `render` renders as `Html`. Remember to always say `Atom Msg` in your code, where `Msg` is your message type. The `Msg` is only needed by the functions you install with `insertMessages`, but they're used in any attribute that needs to invoke your `update` function.

See the [JSON documentation](https://github.com/billstclair/elm-html-template/blob/master/JSON.md) for details about which atom types represent which Elm objects.

unions.name: AttributeFunction
unions.comment: Used to wrap message functions for calling the Html.Attributes functions.

unions.name: Loaders
unions.comment:
`Loaders` stores all the state about loading templates and pages, and the tables for looking them up at `render` time. You should create one in your `init` function by calling `makeLoaders`, initialize it with `insertMessages`, `addOutstandingPagesAndTemplates`, call `loadOutstandingPageOrTemplate` to get a `Cmd` to start loading the pages and templates, then include it in your `Model`.

You should always reference it as `Loaders Msg x`, where `Msg` is your `Msg` type, and `x` is whatever type you decide to use if you need to store state for your template and page loaders. See `getExtra` and `setExtra`.

`Loaders` is opaque to user code, though there are a bunch of functions for querying and modifying it.

aliases.name: HtmlTemplateRecord
aliases.type: { tag : String, attributes : List ( String, Atom msg ), body : List (Atom msg) }

aliases.name: LoadersRecord
aliases.type: { templateLoader : String -> Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg, templatesToLoad : Set String, pageLoader : String -> Loaders msg x -> Cmd msg, pagesToLoad : Set String, dicts : TemplateDicts msg, pageProcessors : Dict String (String -> Atom msg -> Loaders msg x -> Loaders msg x), extra : x }

aliases.name: TemplateDicts
aliases.comment: The dictionaries for looking up atoms (`"$foo"`), templates (`"?foo"`), pages (`"@foo"`, and functions/delayed bindings/messages (`"#foo"`).
aliases.type: { atoms : Dict String (Atom msg), templates : Dict String (Atom msg), pages : Dict String (Atom msg), functions : Dict String (List (Atom msg) -> Dicts msg -> Atom msg), delayedBindingsFunctions : Set String, messages : Dict String (List (Atom msg) -> Dicts msg -> msg) }
